zotero.abstractNote: Reproducibility is a cornerstone of the scientific method. In robotics, however, reproducibility is notoriously difficult to achieve due to the complexity of hardware, the sensitivity of algorithms to environmental conditions, and the lack of standardized experimental setups. To address this, we present a robot cluster for reproducible research in dexterous manipulation. The cluster consists of 10 identical robot setups, each equipped with a high-degree-of-freedom robotic hand and a variety of sensors. We provide a standardized software stack, a set of benchmark tasks, and a cloud-based infrastructure for data collection and experiment management. We demonstrate the utility of the cluster by reproducing several state-of-the-art results in dexterous manipulation and by conducting a large-scale study on the effect of environmental variations on learning performance.
